title: HARM REDUCTION SERVICES - 211info
text: PORTLAND PEOPLES OUTREACH PROJECT HARM REDUCTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides sterile syringes, Naloxone (Narcan), and other harm-reduction supplies directly to users through volunteers doing outreach on foot and bicycles. Unlike many needle exchanges, this is need-based, meaning users do not have to dispose of dirty syringes to receive new ones. Confidential Address Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Friday Bicycle/Van Outreach: 6pm-7pm Eastbank Esplanade at SE Main Street 7:25pm-7:50pm Greyhound/Bud Clark Center at NW 6th and Hoyt Streets 8pm-8:45pm West side; text or call for location Saturday St. Johns: 1pm-4pm The Cut (Peninsula Crossing Trail), on the bike path just west of N. Lombard Street and Macrum Avenue; location sometimes changes, call to verify Sunday Driving and Foot Outreach: 2:30pm-3:15pm Hollywood Transit Center end of NE Weidler off 42nd Avenue 3:45pm-4:30pm South Park Blocks at SW Park and Jefferson 4:45pm-5:30pm Greyhound/Bud Clark at NW 6th and Hoyt Streets Email (503) 765-7767 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 765-7767 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: -Call or visit website for outreach locations, schedules, and information -Visit Facebook for updates and events at www.facebook.com/PortlandPeoplesOutreachProject FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: City of Portland Services: Needle Exchange and Injection Supplies Opioid Antidote Distribution If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. PORTLAND PEOPLES OUTREACH PROJECT
